incident_summary: API gateway experienced severe latency degradation (p99 15.2s vs baseline ~500ms) for approximately 2.5 hours due to database connection pool exhaustion triggered by a configuration change that tripled HTTP client timeout values.
root_cause_summary: Configuration change increased HTTP client timeout from 10s to 30s, causing database connections to be held 3x longer and exhausting the fixed-size connection pool (50 connections).
root_cause_trigger: Deployment at 2025-01-15T14:15:00Z changing HTTP client timeout configuration from 10s to 30s
customer_impact_description: Customers experienced significantly slower API response times, with requests taking up to 15+ seconds instead of the normal sub-second response. Some requests may have timed out entirely if client-side timeouts were shorter than 15 seconds.
affected_functionality: API request processing; All API-dependent operations
resolution_action: Rolled back the configuration change by reverting HTTP client timeout from 30s back to 10s at 2025-01-15T15:45:00Z
resolution_confirmed_by: Connection pool utilization dropped to 60% (from 100%), p99 latency returned to baseline ~500ms by 16:00 UTC, CloudWatch logs showed 'Database connection pool recovered' at 15:05 UTC
data_gaps: Exact customer request failure rate unknown - only latency metrics available, unclear how many requests actually failed vs. succeeded slowly; Geographic/regional scope unconfirmed - data does not indicate whether all regions were affected or only specific deployments; Baseline request volume unknown - cannot determine if traffic spike contributed or if purely configuration-driven; Recovery timing ambiguous - CloudWatch shows pool recovery at 15:05 UTC but rollback deployed at 15:45 UTC, suggesting partial self-recovery before mitigation; Customer-facing product names unknown - only 'api-gateway' service identified, unclear which specific SaaS products depend on this API
